---
title: "Solution 2D — Refactor Day 1 CV with tidymodels"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    code-tools: true
---

**Instructor notes:** Core tidymodels CV is ~10 lines after data prep. Compare to Day 1 for-loop.

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)
library(tidymodels)
library(dplyr)
```

```{r data}
set.seed(123)
n <- 150
gene_names <- c("GeneA", "GeneB", "GeneC", "GeneD", "GeneE", "GeneF", "GeneG", "GeneH", "GeneI", "GeneJ")
gene_data <- as.data.frame(matrix(rnorm(n * 10), nrow = n, dimnames = list(NULL, gene_names)))
gene_data$trait <- -0.5 * gene_data$GeneA + 0.8 * gene_data$GeneB + rnorm(n, sd = 0.3)
```

```{r tidymodels-cv}
set.seed(42)
folds <- vfold_cv(gene_data, v = 5)

rec <- recipe(trait ~ ., data = gene_data) |>
  step_normalize(all_numeric_predictors())

spec <- linear_reg(penalty = tune(), mixture = 1) |>
  set_engine("glmnet") |>
  set_mode("regression")

wf <- workflow() |> add_recipe(rec) |> add_model(spec)

grid <- grid_regular(penalty(range = c(-3, 0)), levels = 5)

tune_res <- tune_grid(
  wf, resamples = folds,
  grid = grid,
  metrics = metric_set(rmse, rsq)
)

collect_metrics(tune_res) |> filter(.metric == "rmse") |> arrange(mean)
```

```{r session, echo=FALSE}
sessionInfo()
```
